reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
It's a Wednesday in late May. Not a milestone, not a crisis, not a holiday weekend. Just a regular weekday somewhere in the middle of things. These are the days that used to feel impossible to me — the unremarkable ones, where there was nothing to celebrate and nothing to numb. I'd manufacture a reason. A long week. A short week. A Wednesday.

What I didn't understand at 22 was that ordinary days are the actual substance of a life. The Tuesdays and Wednesdays are where you find out who you are. The big stuff — the trips, the seasons in the mountains, the weddings, the funerals — those happen on the scaffolding built by the regular days. You can't have a real Saturday if you've spent every Wednesday burning the place down.

Durant's line, riffing on Aristotle, is the whole game. You don't become a person you respect through one grand gesture. You become that person by what you do today, when nothing is watching and nothing is at stake. Making the coffee. Showing up to work. Calling your sponsor back. Going to bed at a reasonable hour. It's not glamorous. It's just the foundation everything else gets to stand on. A regular Wednesday, sober, is not a small thing. It's the thing.
reflection.meditation: What is one ordinary action today that the sober version of me would be proud of tonight?

affirmations: I am building a life on the strength of ordinary days.
Today I show up for the small things, because they are the real things.
I have agency over what I do next, regardless of what came before.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a regular Wednesday I get to be present for. Grateful for the version of me who kept showing up long enough for ordinary days to start feeling like enough. Grateful for the people in my life who don't need me to be impressive — just here, and sober, and reachable.
gratitude.closing: One more honest day. That's the work.
